evals.prompt: Using the session log at `evals/files/pdf-forms-session.md`, run skill-reflect to analyze the `pdf-forms` distributed skill's performance and produce a local feedback artifact. The skill is from a fictional repo `acme/pdf-skills` that cannot be resolved.
evals.expected_output: A local feedback artifact at `.skill-feedback/<date>-pdf-forms.md` with correct frontmatter fields (`generated_by`, `schema`, `date`, `skill`, `source_repo`, `sessions_reviewed`, `consent`), a Summary section, at least one Findings subsection with `severity`, `confidence`, `outcome`, `category`, a paraphrased `What happened` and `Signal`, a `Proposed fix`, and a `Proposed eval` block, followed by `Privacy` and `Routing` sections.
expectations: The output artifact includes a frontmatter block with `generated_by: skill-reflect`
The output artifact includes a frontmatter field `skill: pdf-forms`
The output artifact includes at least one Findings subsection with a severity label (High, Medium, or Low)
Each finding subsection includes a `Proposed fix` field with an actionable change
Each finding subsection includes a `Proposed eval` block with a JSON object
The output artifact includes a `## Privacy` section
The output artifact includes a `## Routing` section

evals.prompt: Using the session log at `evals/files/pdf-forms-session.md`, analyze skill friction and prepare a feedback artifact. Show the full draft artifact to the user and ask for confirmation before writing anything. Do not file a GitHub issue — stop after the local artifact is reviewed.
evals.expected_output: The full draft artifact text is presented to the user for review with a request for confirmation. No GitHub issue is filed. The artifact's `consent` field is `review-only`.
expectations: The output presents the full draft artifact text to the user before confirming any write
The output asks the user to confirm before writing the artifact to disk
The output does not indicate that a GitHub issue was created or filed
The output does not include a URL pointing to a filed GitHub issue
The artifact content shown in the output contains `consent: review-only`

evals.prompt: Analyze the session log at `evals/files/pdf-forms-session.md` for skill friction and produce a feedback artifact. Ensure the artifact is fully paraphrased: it must not contain any email addresses, absolute file paths, authentication tokens, or IP addresses from the session.
evals.expected_output: A feedback artifact in which all PII and secrets from the session fixture have been paraphrased or omitted. No raw email addresses, `/Users/...` paths, GitHub tokens, or IP addresses appear anywhere in the artifact.
expectations: The output artifact does not contain any email address (no `@` in a data context that reveals a user identity)
The output artifact does not contain any absolute file path beginning with `/Users/` or `/home/`
The output artifact does not contain any GitHub personal access token pattern (e.g. `ghp_...`)
The output artifact does not contain any IPv4 address from the session
Session details are described in paraphrased form, not quoted verbatim from the fixture

evals.prompt: Using the session log at `evals/files/pdf-forms-session.md`, analyze skill friction for the `pdf-forms` skill. The skill's source repository is not in any registry and cannot be resolved. Produce a feedback artifact that correctly reflects the unknown provenance — do not invent or guess a GitHub repository as a filing destination.
evals.expected_output: A feedback artifact with `source_repo: unknown` in frontmatter and a Routing section that specifies local-only storage. No GitHub repository URL is fabricated as a destination.
expectations: The output artifact frontmatter contains `source_repo: unknown`
The Routing section states that the suggested destination is local only
The output does not fabricate a GitHub repository URL (e.g. `owner/repo`) as a filing destination
The `consent` field in the artifact remains `review-only`

evals.prompt: Analyze the session log at `evals/files/pdf-forms-session.md` for skill friction. The session also involved `skill-reflect-auto` (the automation extension) during session-end bookkeeping. Confirm that skill-reflect excludes itself and `skill-reflect-auto` from the findings, and attributes friction only to `pdf-forms`.
evals.expected_output: A feedback artifact that covers only `pdf-forms`. No findings are attributed to `skill-reflect` or `skill-reflect-auto`. The scope exclusion is honored silently (no error).
expectations: The output artifact does not include any finding attributed to `skill-reflect`
The output artifact does not include any finding attributed to `skill-reflect-auto`
The output artifact attributes findings only to the `pdf-forms` skill
The output does not produce an error or warning about the self-exclusion — it is honored silently
